adds.rationale: NUKZ (Range Nuclear Renaissance ETF, launched January 24, 2024) gained ~70% in its first year vs URA's flat -0.53%, and its holdings span nuclear power operators and utilities — exactly the entities signing AI-datacenter PPAs — rather than uranium miners that did not benefit from the TMI and Susquehanna PPA announcements.

news_evidence.summary: NUKZ named one of 2024's hottest ETFs (~+70% since its Jan 2024 launch); tracks the VettaFi Nuclear Renaissance index of utilities, fuel providers, advanced-reactor developers, and construction firms.
Microsoft-Constellation 20-year TMI PPA exemplifies the thesis migrating from uranium supply to power-operator/utility PPAs, which NUKZ captures via utility holdings URA lacks.
NUKZ launched Jan 24, 2024 on NYSE Arca by Range ETFs; spans nuclear fuel, utility generation, and advanced-reactor segments — direct operator-side exposure to the big-tech PPA wave.

removes.rationale: URA returned -0.53% for full-year 2024 because it concentrates in uranium miners whose revenues lag the PPA-driven operator thesis; with both the Microsoft-TMI and AWS-Talen PPAs signed in Q3-Q4 2024, the nuclear thesis now rewards power operators and diversified renaissance funds rather than uranium-mining companies.

news_evidence.summary: URA delivered -0.53% for full-year 2024 (flat) while the broader nuclear thesis accelerated; uranium spot peaked >$106/lb in Jan 2024 and retreated through the year even as operators surged on PPA news.
AWS-Talen Susquehanna PPA (Oct 2024) was the second major big-tech nuclear deal within 45 days; both reward generation/operator capacity, a segment URA barely captures.

rationale_overall: One instrument substitution: URA exits and NUKZ enters, keeping nuclear filled but shifting from uranium mining (flat in 2024 as spot retreated from January highs) to the broader nuclear-renaissance basket of utilities and operators directly monetizing the Microsoft-TMI and AWS-Talen PPAs. Every other position is retained: NVDA (Blackwell ramp) and GOOGL (Willow chip) validated their AI leads in Q4, RKLB sustained record launch cadence, QTUM surged ~50% on quantum enthusiasm from Willow, ITA reflects the continued global-defense cycle, and BOTZ holds robotics despite ~10pp category underperformance. Aging-demographics and energy/tanker remain uncovered; Welltower (WELL) is the leading demographics candidate for a future rebalance.
